title: Setup Object
description: Object that represents an existing Setup.

methods.name: activate
methods.description: Sets this object as the default container.

methods.name: additiveContainerByType
methods.description: Returns the additive container with the specified type.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copyAfter
methods.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation in the tree after the given operation. Throws an exception if a not allowed copy operation is made for example copying a operation out of a setup.

methods.name: copyBefore
methods.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation in the tree before the given operation. Throws an exception if a not allowed copy operation is made for example copying a operation out of a setup.

methods.name: copyInto
methods.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation into the given container. You can only copy into containers, such as setups or folders. Copied operation will be copied at the end of all operations already in the container. Throws an exception if a not allowed copy operation is made for example copying a setup into a setup.

methods.name: createFromCAMTemplate
methods.description: **RETIRED** Create new operations, folders, or patterns from the specified CAMTemplate. They are added to the end of the parent setup.

methods.name: createFromCAMTemplate2
methods.description: Create new operations, folders, or patterns from the specified CAMTemplate. They are added to the end of the parent setup.

methods.name: createFromTemplate
methods.description: **RETIRED** Create and add operations, folders or patterns from the specified template file to the end of this setup.

methods.name: createFromTemplateXML
methods.description: **RETIRED** Create and add operations, folders or patterns from the specified template content XML to the end of this setup.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the operation from the document. In case of a setup or folder, all containing child operations will be deleted as well. Note: Child classes may overwrite this function and throw an exception if the object cannot be deleted.

methods.name: duplicate
methods.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation in the tree after the itself.

methods.name: modifyUtility
methods.description: Get ModifyUtility for the current operation by given utility type.

methods.name: moveAfter
methods.description: Move operation in tree after the given operation. Throws an exception if a not allowed move is made for example moving a operation out of a setup.

methods.name: moveBefore
methods.description: Move operation in tree before the given operation. Throws an exception if a not allowed move is made for example moving a operation out of a setup.

methods.name: moveInto
methods.description: Move operation in tree into the given container. This only works with setups, patterns and folders. Moved operation will be moved at the end of all operations already in the container. Throws an exception if a not allowed move is made for example moving a setup into a setup.

properties.name: allOperations
properties.description: Returns an ObjectCollection containing all of the operations in this setup. This includes all operations nested in folders and patterns.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this object.

properties.name: children
properties.description: Returns a collection containing all of the immediate (top level) child operations, folders and patterns in this setup, in the order they appear in the browser.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Returns a message corresponding to any active error associated with the value of this parameter.

properties.name: fixtureEnabled
properties.description: Set this value to enable the use of fixtures for this setup. To then set the fixture models themselves use the `fixtures` property.

properties.name: fixtures
properties.description: Gets and sets the fixtures associated with the setup, which are represented by an ObjectCollection of models, where a model can be an Occurrence, BRepBody, or MeshBody. To be able to set models as fixtures, the fixturesEnabled property has to be set first.

properties.name: folders
properties.description: Returns the Folders collection that provides access to existing folders in this setup.

properties.name: generatedDataCollection
properties.description: Get the generated data associated with a given operation base instance. The type of data depends on the strategy type and might not be available for all strategy types. The available types can be found in GeneratedData.cs

properties.name: hasError
properties.description: Gets if errors were encountered when generating the operation.

properties.name: hasWarning
properties.description: Gets if problems were encountered when generating the operation.

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Gets if this setup is active.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets if this operation is currently visible in the graphics window. Use the isLightBulbOn to change if the eye icon beside the operation node in the browser is on or not. Parent nodes in the browser can have their light bulb off which affects all of their children so this property does not indicate if the operation is actually visible, just that it should be visible if all of its parent nodes are also visible. Use the isVisible property to determine if it's actually visible.

properties.name: isOptional
properties.description: Gets and sets the "Optional" property value of the operation. Gets/sets true if the operation is optional.

properties.name: isProtected
properties.description: Gets and sets the "protected" property value of the operation. Gets/sets true if the operation is protected.

properties.name: isSelected
properties.description: Gets if this operation is selected in the 'Setups' browser.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets the "Suppressed" property value of the operation. Gets/sets true if the operation is suppressed.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets if this operation is currently visible in the graphics window. Use the isLightBulbOn to change if the eye icon beside the operation node in the browser is on or not. Parent nodes in the browser can have their light bulb off which affects all of their children. This property indicates the final result and whether this operation is actually visible or not.

properties.name: machine
properties.description: Gets and sets the Machine associated with the setup. The returned Machine is a transient copy, so the Machine needs to be set to the Setup again to apply any changes.

properties.name: models
properties.description: Gets and sets the input models associated with the setup. Passing in an empty ObjectCollection will remove all current inputs. Valid collection items are Occurrence, BRepBody, or MeshBody.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the operation as seen in the browser. This name is unique as compared to the names of all other operations in the document.

properties.name: notes
properties.description: Gets and sets the notes of the operation.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: operationId
properties.description: Returns the id of the operation. This id is unique as compared to the ids of all other operations in the document. This id will not change when changing the order or parent of the operation. This id will remain valid when the document is saved and reloaded.

properties.name: operations
properties.description: Returns the Operations collection that provides access to existing operations in this setup.

properties.name: operationType
properties.description: Gets the Operation Type. It can be MillingOperation, TurningOperation, JetOperation or AdditiveOperation.

properties.name: parameters
properties.description: Gets the CAMParameters collection for this operation.

properties.name: parentSetup
properties.description: Gets the Setup this operation belongs to.

properties.name: patterns
properties.description: Returns the Patterns collection that provides access to existing patterns in this setup.

properties.name: printSetting
properties.description: Gets and sets the PrintSetting associated with the setup.

properties.name: stockMaterial
properties.description: Gets/ and sets the Stock material associated with the setup.

properties.name: stockMode
properties.description: Gets and sets the bodies associated with the setup. Passing in an empty ObjectCollection will remove all current bodies. Valid input is MeshBody and/or BRepBody objects.

properties.name: stockSolids
properties.description: Gets and sets the stock solids associated with the setup, which are represented by an ObjectCollection of models, where a model can be an Occurrence, BRepBody, or MeshBody. StockMode has to be set to `SolidStock` otherwise this will throw an error.

properties.name: strategy
properties.description: Gets the name of the strategy associated with this operation.

properties.name: visibilityManager
properties.description: Visibility manager for this setup.

properties.name: warning
properties.description: Returns a message corresponding to any active warning associated with the value of this parameter.

properties.name: workCoordinateSystem
properties.description: Gets the Work Coordinate System associated with the setup as 4x4 matrix. From Matrix3D, Orientation and Origin data can be fetched.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.name: CAM Parameter Modification API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates changing parameters of existing toolpaths.

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

related_samples.name: Generate Setup Sheets API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the setup sheets for an existing toolpath..

related_samples.name: Generate Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Create CAM Operation From Template API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates adding a new toolpath into the document using an existing CAM template. You can view the template here, although it will be loaded automatically. Run the sample script within any Fusion project containing at least one setup. A new facing operation will be created at the end of each setup discovered.

related_samples.name: Post Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates posting toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Set Vise Origin As Setup WCS Origin API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to define our setup WCS origin relative to our vise origin from either a component, a sketch point or a joint origin.The Work Coordinate System is a reference point set in our CAM workspace and on our machine. All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

activate.title: Setup.activate Method
activate.description: Sets this object as the default container.

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.activate()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->activate();

activate.return_description: Returns true if the activation was successful.

additiveContainerByType.title: Setup.additiveContainerByType Method
additiveContainerByType.description: Returns the additive container with the specified type.

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.additiveContainerByType(containerType)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->additiveContainerByType(containerType);

additiveContainerByType.return_description: Returns the specified container or null in the case where there is no container with the specified type.

classType.title: Setup.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.cam.Setup.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::cam::Setup::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copyAfter.title: Setup.copyAfter Method
copyAfter.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation in the tree after the given operation. Throws an exception if a not allowed copy operation is made for example copying a operation out of a setup.

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.copyAfter(operation)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->copyAfter(operation);

copyAfter.return_description: Returns if copy command was successful.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Operation to copy targeted operation after.

copyBefore.title: Setup.copyBefore Method

#include

copyInto.title: Setup.copyInto Method
copyInto.description: Creates a duplicate of the operation into the given container. You can only copy into containers, such as setups or folders. Copied operation will be copied at the end of all operations already in the container. Throws an exception if a not allowed copy operation is made for example copying a setup into a setup.

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.copyInto(container)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->copyInto(container);

copyInto.return_description: Returns if copy command was successful.

parameters.name: container
parameters.description: Container to copy targeted operation into.

createFromCAMTemplate.title: Setup.createFromCAMTemplate Method
createFromCAMTemplate.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Create new operations, folders, or patterns from the specified CAMTemplate. They are added to the end of the parent setup. Remarks

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.createFromCAMTemplate(camTemplate)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->createFromCAMTemplate(camTemplate);

createFromCAMTemplate.return_description: Returns an array containing all of the operations, folders and patterns created from the template.

parameters.name: camTemplate
parameters.description: The CAMTemplate object to use to create the new operation, folder, or pattern.

createFromCAMTemplate2.title: Setup.createFromCAMTemplate2 Method
createFromCAMTemplate2.description: Create new operations, folders, or patterns from the specified CAMTemplate. They are added to the end of the parent setup.

syntax.python: returnValue = setup_var.createFromCAMTemplate2(input)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = setup_var->createFromCAMTemplate2(input);

createFromCAMTemplate2.return_description: Returns an array containing all of the operations, folders and patterns created from the template.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Input object that contains the template to create from and the generation mode.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing SLA API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive SLA manufacturing setup.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive setup using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select a SLA 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically oriented and arranged within the build area of the selected SLA machine. This script will also create support structures, based on the orientation of each component.

The support and orientation operations are created from a template. The script further demonstrates how to wrap script code into a command such that only one undo entry is created for the entire script instead of one entry per internal action.

related_samples.name: Create CAM Operation From Template API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates adding a new toolpath into the document using an existing CAM template. You can view the template here, although it will be loaded automatically. Run the sample script within any Fusion project containing at least one setup. A new facing operation will be created at the end of each setup discovered.

createFromTemplate.title: Setup.createFromTemplate Method
createFromTemplate.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Create and add operations, folders or patterns from the specified template file to the end of this setup. Remarks

syntax.cpp:

createFromTemplate.return_description: Returns the collection containing all of the operations, folders and patterns created from the template file.

parameters.description: The full path to the template file.

parameters.name: templateXML
parameters.description: The full XML content of the template.

For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

notes.title: Setup.notes Property
notes.description: Gets and sets the notes of the operation.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.notes

# Set the value of the property.
setup_var.notes = propertyValue

objectType.title: Setup.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.objectType

operationId.title: Setup.operationId Property
operationId.description: Returns the id of the operation. This id is unique as compared to the ids of all other operations in the document. This id will not change when changing the order or parent of the operation. This id will remain valid when the document is saved and reloaded.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.operationId

operationType.title: Setup.operationType Property
operationType.description: Gets the Operation Type. It can be MillingOperation, TurningOperation, JetOperation or AdditiveOperation.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.operationType

operations.title: Setup.operations Property
operations.description: Returns the Operations collection that provides access to existing operations in this setup.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.operations

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

related_samples.name: Basic Milling Workflow Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the creation of a basic milling workflow from scriptDemonstrates creating a setup, searching tool library to retrieve a tool, create a couple of machining operations and a NC program, ready for post processing.Use the 2D Strategies model from the Fusion CAM Samples folder as your CAD model.

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

related_samples.name: Wood Routing Workflow Sample
related_samples.description: This script demonstrates routing wood panels.When running the sample, it assumes you have an open design containing one or more "panels" oriented flat in the X-Y plane. The script creates a setup and a 2D contour operation with tabs to route the panels from a standard sheet.

parameters.title: Setup.parameters Property
parameters.description: Gets the CAMParameters collection for this operation.

syntax.python:

related_samples.name: Set Vise Origin As Setup WCS Origin API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to define our setup WCS origin relative to our vise origin from either a component, a sketch point or a joint origin.The Work Coordinate System is a reference point set in our CAM workspace and on our machine. All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.

It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

stockSolids.title: Setup.stockSolids Property
stockSolids.description: Gets and sets the stock solids associated with the setup, which are represented by an ObjectCollection of models, where a model can be an Occurrence, BRepBody, or MeshBody. StockMode has to be set to `SolidStock` otherwise this will throw an error.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = setup_var.stockSolids

# Set the value of the property.
setup_var.stockSolids = propertyValue

All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.
